summary: Merged a large fighter header/struct cleanup while deliberately moving unfinished fighter.c progress back to assembly so the shared Fighter definitions could land without further branch divergence. The PR expanded and renamed many Fighter fields, added typed baselib JObj/GObj-related declarations, updated downstream fighter character code to use the new member names and callback grouping, added src/melee/ft/fighter.c.o to build lists, and renamed func_8006B82C to Fighter_procUpdate in assembly references. Review specifically corrected an attempted inner phys struct in Fighter: an ftlib assertion showed the physical velocity/position fields should remain directly in Fighter rather than nested.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/ft/fighter.h substantially, replacing broad filler ranges and older ambiguous member names with more explicit offset-based Fighter fields.

Updated many Fighter velocity/position field names from camel/mixed forms to lower_snake offset names, for example x80_selfVel became x80_self_vel and xEC_groundVel became xEC_ground_vel in Marth/Mars special move code.

Grouped Fighter callback fields under a cb member in call sites, for example x21D4_callback_EnterHitlag, x21D8_callback_ExitHitlag, x21BC_callback_Accessory4, and x21EC_callback became accessed as ft->cb.x21D4_callback_EnterHitlag and similar.
Renamed fighter bone joint accessors in client code from x0_joint to x0_jobj, matching the HSD_JObj type concept used by baselib.

Added or improved function prototypes in include/functions.h with concrete argument and return types, including HSD_JObj pointers, CameraBox return type, Vec arguments, HSD_GObj return type, and item kind naming via itGetKind.
Added many definitions to src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h and adjusted src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h, supporting more accurate typed use of JObj/GObj structures.
Renamed assembly symbol func_8006B82C to Fighter_procUpdate and updated references in asm/melee/ft/fighter.s and asm/melee/ft/code_800877F8.s.
Added src/melee/ft/fighter.c.o to both obj_files.mk and e_files.mk while moving unfinished decomp work back into fighter.s, per the PR body.
Touched multiple fighter character source files, especially ftMars special move files, to compile against the revised Fighter field layout and names.
Changed asmdiff.sh file mode to executable in the diff excerpt, though this was not highlighted in the PR discussion.
smart_moves: Separated shareable header/struct progress from unfinished function decompilation by moving incomplete fighter.c work back to fighter.s, allowing Fighter definitions to merge earlier and reduce branch divergence.
Kept offset prefixes in field names while improving semantic readability, preserving decomp safety and searchability during an evolving struct layout.
Propagated header field renames through active C users instead of leaving compatibility aliases, forcing call sites to reflect the new struct organization consistently.
Used typed baselib concepts such as HSD_JObj and HSD_GObj in prototypes and member names, which helps future matching by reducing void*/u8* ambiguity.
Accepted review evidence from an existing ftlib assertion rather than optimizing purely for local organization, preventing an inaccurate nested Fighter physical-state struct from landing.
decomp_lessons: Do not introduce inner structs into major game structs merely for readability when offset assertions or known access patterns indicate the original layout is flat.
For Fighter fields, offset-based names such as x80_self_vel remain useful even when semantics are known, because many call sites and matching constraints still depend on exact layout.
Large header refactors should be paired with mechanical call-site updates in character files so the build remains coherent across fighter-specific code.
When function bodies are not ready to match, it can be better to keep them in assembly and still land the recovered headers/types that unblock other work.
Assembly labels can be renamed to meaningful symbols without decompiling the function body, as shown by func_8006B82C to Fighter_procUpdate, provided all assembly references are updated.

Prototype improvements in include/functions.h are valuable matching infrastructure: replacing untyped u8*/void declarations with HSD_JObj*, HSD_GObj*, Vec*, and concrete return types reduces casts and clarifies later decompilation.
Reviewers may use existing assertions in C files as evidence for struct layout decisions; future struct proposals should check those assertions before adding nested structs or padding changes.
naming_conventions: Fighter physical vector names moved toward lower_snake_case with offset prefixes, for example x80_self_vel, x74_anim_vel, x8c_kb_vel, x98_atk_shield_kb, and xEC_ground_vel.
HSD joint object fields should use jobj terminology rather than generic joint when the member is an HSD_JObj pointer, as in x0_jobj.
Fighter callback members were accessed through a cb grouping, with original offset-bearing callback names retained below that grouping.
Meaningful fighter symbols may use Fighter_ prefix, as in Fighter_procUpdate replacing func_8006B82C.
Known item functions can be renamed from raw address names to semantic names while preserving comments, shown by func_8026B300 becoming itGetKind.
Offset comments and x-prefixed member names remained part of the style for partially understood structs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved unfinished fighter.c decompilation back to fighter.s to avoid merging unstable or nonmatching C while still landing header/type progress.
Renamed a raw assembly global label func_8006B82C to Fighter_procUpdate and updated lis/addi symbol references in assembly call setup sites.
Added fighter.c.o to build object lists while keeping fighter.s present, indicating staged migration of fighter code from assembly to C.
Used header and prototype typing to improve C matching context across existing character files without necessarily changing behavior.
Kept exact offsets in Fighter field names and comments to protect layout-sensitive matching.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned the motivation for an inner phys struct and cited an ftlib.c assertion as evidence that there should not be an inner struct there.

review_feedback.author: snuffysasa
review_feedback.feedback: Agreed that the inner phys struct was likely wrong, explaining it had been for organization and inspired by one of Kalua's matches, but the assertion indicated no inner struct and the correct variable name.

review_feedback.author: snuffysasa
review_feedback.feedback: PR body stated unfinished work was moved from fighter.c back to fighter.s so fighter.h and structs could merge without the branch diverging as much.

review_feedback.author: snuffysasa
review_feedback.feedback: Issue comment noted an update with an error pointed out by r-burns; the exact error beyond the shown inner-phys-struct discussion is not fully evidenced in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Inspect the final merged src/melee/ft/fighter.h from PR #336 to confirm whether the inner phys struct was fully removed and which exact Fighter field names landed.
Search later PRs for Fighter_procUpdate to see when func_8006B82C was eventually decompiled or further renamed.
Search for cb.x21D4_callback_EnterHitlag and related callback fields to understand the final Fighter callback substructure convention.
Search for x0_jobj in fighter bone structs to verify the complete HSD_JObj typing convention across all characters.
Review ftlib.c assertions around Fighter layout before proposing additional nested structs or padding changes.
